title: Browser transport adapter for distribution sync
purpose: Deliver the browser transport adapter that enables distribution sync between WASM and native haematite nodes over WebSocket or WebRTC data channels. The sync protocol payload is identical to native — only the transport layer differs.
task: Implement a transport adapter that bridges haematite's distribution sync protocol (DIST-001) to browser-compatible transports: WebSocket for server-to-browser sync and WebRTC data channels for browser-to-browser sync. The sync protocol payload (root hash exchange, tree walk, node transfer) is identical to native. The adapter translates between beamr distribution messages and WebSocket/WebRTC frames. Does not implement the sync protocol itself (DIST-001) or merge logic (DIST-002).

requirements.title: WebSocket transport
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL implement a WebSocket transport that carries haematite sync protocol messages between a browser and a server. THE SYSTEM SHALL serialise sync messages into WebSocket binary frames. THE SYSTEM SHALL deserialise incoming frames back into sync messages.
acceptance: The WebSocket transport connects to a server endpoint
Sync protocol messages are serialised into binary WebSocket frames
Incoming frames are deserialised into sync protocol messages
A full pull-sync completes over WebSocket

requirements.title: Protocol payload parity
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL use identical sync protocol payloads in the browser transport as in the native distribution transport. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT introduce browser-specific message formats. The only difference is the transport layer — WebSocket instead of TCP.
acceptance: The sync message format is identical between native and WASM builds
A native node can sync with a WASM node via WebSocket
Root hash exchange, tree walk, and node transfer messages are byte-compatible

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement the sync protocol — that is DIST-001
SHALL NOT implement merge or consistency modes — that is DIST-002
SHALL NOT implement a WebRTC signaling server — signaling is application-level
SHALL NOT implement the IndexedDB NodeStore or OPFS WAL — those are WASM-001 and WASM-002
SHALL NOT implement WebRTC data channel transport — deferred to future phase. WebSocket only for MVP.
verification: cargo check -p haematite --target wasm32-unknown-unknown --features wasm succeeds
cargo test -p haematite --lib passes all tests (native)
cargo clippy -p haematite produces no errors
No file in crates/haematite/src/wasm/ exceeds 500 lines
